name: Cayman Islands (United Kingdom)

colors.symbolism: the field of the British Blue Ensign
the saltire and cross of the Union Flag canton; waves and stars in the Cayman coat of arms
the cross of St George and saltire of St Patrick in the Union Flag canton; the lion in the chief of the coat of arms
the sea around the islands depicted on the coat of arms

description: A British Blue Ensign — navy field with the Union Flag in the upper-hoist canton — defaced in the fly with the coat of arms of the Cayman Islands: a shield with three green stars on wavy lines and a red chief bearing a gold lion, surmounted by a sea-turtle and pineapple over the motto 'He hath founded it upon the seas'.
history: The current design was adopted on 14 May 1958 (with the coat of arms granted by Queen Elizabeth II) and elevated to a national flag (rather than just an ensign) on 6 February 1999, when the disc surrounding the badge was removed and the badge enlarged. It is used in addition to the Union Flag.

features.symbolism: the field of the British Blue Ensign, denoting the Cayman Islands' status as a British Overseas Territory
the Cayman Islands' status as a British Overseas Territory

features.role: coat of arms of the Cayman Islands
features.symbolism: a shield bearing three green stars on wavy bands of white and blue (representing the three islands rising from the sea), with a red chief showing a gold lion passant guardant for ties to the United Kingdom; above is a green sea-turtle on a coil of rope and a yellow pineapple, with the motto 'He hath founded it upon the seas' from Psalm 24
